title: Exceptions should be avoided
description: While exceptions are a common feature of modern languages, there are several reasons to potentially avoid them: They make the control flow of a program more difficult to understand because they introduce additional hidden exit points. It is difficult to introduce them gradually in a codebase that was not designed with exceptions in mind. They add to the size of each binary produced, thereby increasing both compile time and final executable size.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.
